title: CloudFront Cache Keys, Origin Requests, TTLs, and Invalidation · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for cloudfront cache keys, origin requests, ttls, and invalidation and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for cache-key, forwarding, and freshness contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.
Use origin request policy for additional required viewer data without unnecessary cache fragmentation or trust escalation.
Combine origin Cache-Control, minimum, default, maximum, stale behavior, validators, and error caching with content-change and privacy requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 4741 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Compression Vary
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Compression Vary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 9739 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Compression Vary.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Compression Vary, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: CloudFront compression reduces every file type and never changes cache identity.
